element_introduction_order.mastery_context: close-quarters fallback when out of ammunition
primary ranged weapon for early levels
improved damage output and fire rate
rapid-fire weapon for handling multiple threats
hidden rooms containing treasure, health, ammunition
particularly difficult enemies ending each episode
primary objective to complete each level
optional collection for points and completion bonuses

typical_relief_triggers: finding health pickup after enemy encounter
killing final boss to complete episode
typical_tension_triggers: entering unknown area with multiple exits
approaching locked door requiring key search
facing boss encounter at episode finale
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced action with cyclical tension-relief tied to enemy encounters and resource management. Intensity increases toward episode finales with boss fights. Secret areas provide optional relief and resource replenishment.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Wall tiles that slide back when activated reveal hidden rooms containing treasure, health, or ammunition
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Activatable wall tiles signal hidden areas; secret areas are purely optional and reward exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gradual introduction of basic enemies (guards) and weapon types (knife, pistol); focus on level navigation and learning controls
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of faster enemies (dogs/rats), upgraded weapons (submachine gun), locked door mechanics requiring key collection; increased enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Heaviest firepower available (chain gun), toughest boss encounters at episode finales, most complex level layouts
notable_spikes: Episode finale boss fights (particularly difficult single-enemy encounters)
Transition from submachine gun to chain gun availability signals late-game intensity increase

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divided into episodes containing multiple standard levels culminating in a boss-fight level. SNES version contains 30 levels total across 6 episodes (2 secret levels)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Each episode finale (levels 10, 20, 30, etc. in original structure)

notable_level_patterns.description: All levels are flat planes divided into areas and rooms by grid-based walls and doors of equal height. Player must traverse maze to locate elevator exit
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All standard levels follow this structure

notable_level_patterns.description: Hidden rooms accessible via activated wall tiles; reward-driven exploration offering treasure, health, ammunition, and completion bonuses without mandatory progression impact
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Present in all levels per gameplay description

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level themed after Nazi bunkers and buildings; consistent aesthetic supports thematic immersion and enemy type expectations

sources_used: tcrf - Wolfenstein 3D (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
